speaking of discarding effects.
Which would be preferable to standardize to?

For the discard effect, there's
1. "`Discard your hand`", or
2. "`Discard all the cards in your hand`"

And for the redraw effect there's
A. "`then draw that many cards`"
B. "`then draw cards equal to the number of cards discarded`" 
C. "`then draw the same number of cards`"
D. "`then draw an equal amount of cards`"

and to further discuss, because I never claimed to be a *professional* in grammar studies... (using my preferred (a.) for example);
a. "`then draw that many cards plus 2.`"
b. "`then draw 2 plus that many cards.`"
in a perfect world I feel like 2 B a would be optimal but unfortunately we live in a world with textbox size limits
so realistically 1 C a probably
I think 1A or 1C.

discard your hand sounds like very litral...
*removing hands like lego*
I kind of prefer 1A just because it's shorter.

Magic: the Gathering uses two different templates across its rules text, always (for good reason) using a pair of these that refers directly and clearly to a number (of cards):
1B. Discard your hand, then draw cards equal to the number of cards discarded this way.
2A. Discard all the cards in your hand, then draw that many cards.

Personally, since this game doesn't need a strict 700-page encyclopedia of legalese on how cards need to be written, I heavily, *heavily* prefer the much shorter and simpler 1A.
